race.name: La Stelvio Santini
race.tagline: Alpine legend: conquer the Mortirolo and Passo dello Stelvio in Italy's most celebrated granfondo

climate.overview: Alpine June conditions with variable mountain weather. Expect crisp air and sunshine, but prepare for rapid weather changes including potential snow and cold temperatures at elevation.

terrain.overview: High Alpine terrain featuring two of Europe's most iconic climbs: the Passo dello Stelvio and Mortirolo. Routes traverse spectacular mountain scenery with steep gradients, technical descents, and sustained elevation gain.

route_options.name: Short Route
route_options.description: Via Sondalo; accessible entry point suited for developing riders and e-bikes

route_options.name: Medium Route
route_options.description: Via Teglio; intermediate challenge featuring sustained Alpine climbing

route_options.name: Long Route
route_options.description: Via Teglio and Mortirolo; complete Alpine experience with both legendary climbs

adventure.explanation: Thrilling climbs on iconic passes like Stelvio and Mortirolo through stunning alpine scenery offer high adventure, enhanced by a new gravel option.[1][2]

altitude.explanation: High mountain passes provide significant altitude, but multiple route options allow choices based on fitness levels.[1][3]

climate.explanation: June alpine weather can be crisp and sunny but unpredictable, with potential for poor conditions even in summer.[5]

community.explanation: Strong sense of camaraderie from thousands of international riders, couple registration, and support services fosters a vibrant community.[1][4]

elevation.explanation: Routes boast massive elevation gains up to 4,577m on the long course, epitomizing epic climbing challenges.[3]

expenses.explanation: Entry fees range from 90-130 Euros plus additional costs for travel, lodging, and services make it relatively pricey.[5]

experience.explanation: Well-organized event with multiple routes suited to various fitness levels delivers a memorable cycling experience.[1]

field_depth.explanation: Attracts 3,000 dedicated amateur cyclists from Europe and beyond, creating a competitive and skilled peloton.[5]

length.explanation: Varied distances from 44-151km across routes satisfy demands for both shorter challenges and ultra-long epics.[2][3]

logistics.explanation: Efficient organization with start/finish in Bormio, bike services, feed stations, and clear route options ensures smooth logistics.[6]

prestige.explanation: Ranked top 10 worldwide with legendary climbs raced by Giro d'Italia greats, though recent rebrand slightly tempers historic prestige.[1][7]

race_quality.explanation: Exceptional quality from iconic routes, professional organization, custom bibs, and timing chip elevates it to elite sportive status.[7]

technicality.explanation: Demanding ascents and descents on famed passes like Stelvio require solid technical descending and climbing skills.[1][7]

value.explanation: Premium experience with high prestige and challenge, but elevated costs for entry and logistics limit overall value proposition.[5]

biased_opinion.overview: La Stelvio Santini represents the pinnacle of Alpine granfondo racing. The dual ascent of Mortirolo and Passo dello Stelvio in a single day is a bucket-list experience for serious cyclists, offering unmatched climbing pedigree and mountain scenery. The event's three-route structure democratizes participation while maintaining elite challenge. Logistics are solid, though the remote location and June timing require careful planning. For cyclists seeking legendary terrain and international community, this is essential.

course_description.character: Epic Alpine granfondo featuring two of cycling's most iconic and historically significant climbs. The event combines sustained elevation gain, technical descents, and high-altitude mountain passages that echo the routes of Giro d'Italia legends. All routes start and finish in Bormio town center.

suffering_zones.label: Mortirolo ascent: relentless gradient and altitude stress on medium and long routes
Passo dello Stelvio final climb: sustained steep sections with thin air and exposed terrain
Teglio approach: grinding mid-ride elevation gain before major climbs
High-altitude descents: technical and exposed, demanding focus and bike handling

course_description.signature_challenge: The combined Mortirolo-Stelvio double on the long route, replicating one of the Giro d'Italia's most brutal mountain stages. Riders face back-to-back legendary climbs with minimal recovery, testing both physical capacity and mental resilience.

logistics.overview: Event based in Bormio, a well-established Alpine cycling destination in northern Italy. Start and finish in town center. Multiple accommodation and support options available. Nearest major airports require 2-4 hour transfers.
logistics.accommodation: Bormio offers range from budget to luxury options. Bikehotel Funivia and QC Terme are mentioned as partner accommodations with cycling-specific amenities including bike storage, repair facilities, and massage services. Book early as event attracts 3,500 riders.
logistics.registration_notes: Registration €90-130 depending on entry timing. Includes custom bib with name, timing chip, and race package. Couple registration available with special prizes for pairs finishing within 2 minutes of each other. E-bikes permitted on short route only. Age minimum 16 overall, 18 for long route. Medical certificate may be required.

history.overview: La Stelvio Santini (formerly Granfondo Stelvio Santini) is one of Italy's most celebrated granfondo events, rebranded in 2024. The event has grown to rank among the top 10 long-distance cycling events worldwide, attracting increasing numbers of international participants annually. The granfondo showcases the legendary Mortirolo and Passo dello Stelvio climbs, which gained iconic status through Giro d'Italia history, particularly the brutal 2012 stage featuring both ascents.
notable_moments: Rebranding to La Stelvio Santini in 2024 with route modifications due to roadworks
Introduction of gravel route variant in 2026, expanding event diversity
Consistent growth to 3,000-3,500 annual participants from across Europe and beyond
Recognition as top-10 global granfondo, cementing prestige among international cycling community
Couple registration category with special finish-line prizes, adding inclusive social dimension

guide_variables.best_for: Experienced cyclists seeking legendary Alpine climbing; international riders pursuing top-tier granfondo experiences; Giro d'Italia enthusiasts wanting to ride historic routes
guide_variables.avoid_if: Beginner cyclists (except short route); those uncomfortable with high altitude or exposed descents; riders seeking flat or rolling terrain
guide_variables.training_focus: High-altitude climbing endurance; sustained power on 8-12% grades; technical descent skills; mental resilience for back-to-back climbs on long route
guide_variables.packing_essentials: Layered clothing (mountain weather changes rapidly); rain jacket; high-SPF sunscreen; altitude-appropriate nutrition; repair kit; lights for early June sunrise start
guide_variables.timing_strategy: Start early to maximize daylight on long route; pace Mortirolo conservatively to preserve energy for final Stelvio ascent; utilize feed stations strategically

non_negotiables: Ability to climb sustained 8-12% grades for 30+ minutes
Comfort with high-altitude riding (2,758m/9,049ft at Stelvio pass)
Technical descent skills on narrow Alpine roads
Mental fortitude for back-to-back legendary climbs

final_verdict.one_liner: La Stelvio Santini is an elite Alpine granfondo that delivers legendary climbing, international prestige, and bucket-list mountain scenery in a well-organized, accessible format.
final_verdict.full_verdict: La Stelvio Santini ranks among the world's premier granfondo events, justified by its dual ascent of the Mortirolo and Passo dello Stelvio—two of cycling's most storied climbs. The event excels in climb quality (5/5), scenery (5/5), prestige (5/5), and international appeal (5/5). Three route options democratize participation while maintaining elite challenge. Organization is professional with solid logistics, mechanical support, and feed stations. The June timing offers favorable Alpine weather, though mountain conditions remain unpredictable. Primary drawbacks are travel costs to remote Bormio location and registration fees (€90-130) that don't include accommodation. For serious cyclists seeking legendary terrain, international community, and unmatched climbing pedigree, La Stelvio Santini is essential. Tier T1 event with 88/100 overall score.

citations.title: Santini Gran Fondo Stelvio Weekender - Grand Tours Project
La Stelvio Santini - Gran Fondo Guide

research_metadata.data_completeness: High. Core race details (date, location, routes, distances, elevation, participant count) confirmed across multiple sources. Founder and exact founding year not available in sources.
research_metadata.source_conflicts: Route distances vary slightly across sources (short: 44-64km, medium: 108-137.9km, long: 130-151.3km). Long route elevation reported as 4,000m, 4,270m, and 4,577m depending on source. Used most detailed figures (151.3km, 4,577m) from Grand Tours Project.
research_metadata.confidence_level: Very High for 2026 event details, logistics, and course characteristics. Moderate for historical founding details and specific notable moments.
